danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile craft on screen during flip-screen traversal
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat requiring evasion or destruction
Persistent threat that cannot be outrun; requires strategic positioning or defensive measures
Area denial hazards; positioned threats that control screen zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Obstacles moving in predictable patterns that cannot be destroyed
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards requiring timing and precise navigation rather than combat
Pressure mechanic; world-state of urgency and resource scarcity (temporal)

safety_signals.visual_form: Items dropped by defeated pirate ships or scattered in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety enhancement; player agency in risk mitigation
Enhanced defensive capability; extends player threat envelope backward
Damage buffer and area control tool; proxy for player protection
Active protection state; temporary invulnerability or damage reduction
Offensive safety through eliminating threats before contact

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player-controlled Cybernoid ship (allied faction marker through protagonist role)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Storage depots and Federation military infrastructure as protected spaces

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Pirate ships (primary hostile visual form); enemies drop loot when destroyed
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Raided storage depots; planetary defense systems under pirate control; stolen merchandise scattered across levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Storage depots emptied; stolen minerals, jewels, ammunition visible in enemy-controlled zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cybernoid must retrieve and transport booty; enemies actively defend stolen goods

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cybernoid ship deployed as primary military response; progression through pirate-held territory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time-limited mission structure; increasing enemy density and complexity across three levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen-by-screen environmental change showing different defensive configurations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hazards shift from pure enemy encounters to missile swarms to turret fields to navigation puzzles; requires tactical adaptation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Time limit status; collected booty counter; level progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temporal pressure increases as timer decreases; player forced toward riskier engagement strategies

environmental_storytelling.example: Stolen minerals, jewels, and ammunition visible in pirate-controlled zones; player must physically collect and transport these to tell the story of Federation recovery
Different screen configurations (missile swarms vs. turrets vs. fixed obstacles) imply different planetary defense strategies and pirate operational doctrine
Three-level structure with increasing complexity suggests deeper penetration into pirate stronghold; outer zones easier, inner zones heavily fortified
Limited power-up frequency reinforces Federation resource constraints and survival mission framing

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrol screen; engage Cybernoid on sight; drop loot on destruction
Track player position; persistent threat requiring active evasion
Fire in fixed arcs or patterns; immobile; requires navigation around kill zones
Move in fixed predictable paths; cannot be destroyed; require precise timing to navigate past
Enhanced offensive/defensive capability; visual indicator of temporary advantage state

notes: Cybernoid uses flip-screen architecture rather than scrolling, which creates discrete tactical zones. Each screen is a contained threat landscape, making environmental variation a primary communication method for world-state. The game's core manifestation strategy relies on hazard type variation rather than color/faction visual coding—enemy identity is functionally communicated through behavior pattern rather than cosmetic marking. The time-limit mechanic is a critical world-state signal, converting narrative urgency (Federation mission timeframe) into direct player pressure. Political context (Federation vs. Pirates) is established through narrative briefing rather than visual in-game indicators.
